intro.line: Before you open anything else, let me take you all the way back to the beginning — to the little girl in these photos, through every birthday and every year that made you, all the way to the day our roads crossed and forever began.

copy.calligraphyKicker: for the one who makes everything beautiful
copy.countdownMessage:
Another year of loving you begins right now.
You make every single day feel like magic.

paragraphs: Before I show you everything I've made, I need you to know something.

From the moment we met on 7th March 2022, my world changed colour. Every day since then has been a little more beautiful, a little more full, a little more ours. And now, on your birthday, I want to pause everything — just for a moment — and tell you what you mean to me.

You are the reason I believe in forever. Not the forever in stories, but the real kind — the one made of small moments: your laugh over morning chai, your hand reaching for mine in a crowd, the way you look at a sunset like it's performing just for you.

I've built you a little world tonight — your whole story, Nidhi. Every card ahead is a piece of you: from the tiny girl in those first photos, through every birthday and every year that shaped you, all the way to the life we're building now. And at the end of it all, there's something waiting just for you.
So take your time. Explore. Remember. Smile.
This is my love letter to you, Nidhi — not in words alone, but in every place we've ever been, every plate we've shared, every moment that made us _us_.

finale.bdayNoteLabel: A birthday wish, handwritten from my heart

login.line: This birthday surprise is locked away just for you, my love. Sign in to step inside. 💛

memories.name: The Day You Arrived
memories.teaser: Before us, before everything — there was you.
memories.message: Before me, before us, before a single page of our story was written — there was you. A tiny, perfect girl who arrived and quietly changed the world for everyone who would ever love you. I wasn't there yet, Nidhi, but when I look at these photos I feel something fierce and overwhelming: gratitude. Gratitude that you were born, that you made it here, that somewhere in the world a little girl was beginning a life that would one day become my whole world. This is where your story truly begins. Happy birthday, my love. 🤍

photos.caption: The very first you — brand new to a world that had no idea how lucky it had just become.
Lifted to the sky, already reaching for everything. Some things never change. 🤍
A little flower among the flowers — you fit right in. 🌸

memories.name: Little You
memories.teaser: Tiny hands, big eyes, a heart already too big for the frame.
memories.message: Look at you. Tiny hands, enormous eyes, and a personality already far too big for any photograph to hold. Dressed like royalty, fed sweets like a little princess, blowing out candles with your eyes squeezed shut — you've been the centre of everyone's love, the birthday girl, since the very beginning. I love that I get to see these little pieces of you, the years long before I knew you existed. Every one of them was quietly building the woman I would fall completely, hopelessly in love with.

photos.caption: Dressed like a little queen — and already looking at the world like she owned it. 👑
Sweet tooth from the very start — being fed by hands that adored you. 🍬
Eyes closed, wish ready, cake in front of you — you've always known how to make a wish count. 🎂
Party hat on, horn blowing — the original birthday girl. Today is no different. 🎉

memories.name: Where You Come From
memories.teaser: The love you were raised in.
memories.message: Every bit of warmth you carry, every way you turn a house into a home, every ounce of the fierce love you give so freely — it all started here, with these people. Your family. The ones who raised you, fed you, lifted you to the sky and caught you every single time. When I married you, Nidhi, I didn't just fall in love with you — I fell in love with where you come from, and with all the love that quietly shaped you into the woman I adore. And the best part? They're still right here — at every celebration, every hug, every photo. Some things only grow stronger with time. 💗

photos.caption: Toes in the water, your whole family laughing — the kind of day a childhood is made of. 🌊
Generations in a single frame — the love you were raised in.
In your grandmother's lap — wrapped in the oldest, softest kind of love.
Safe in the arms that held you first.
Everyday home, everyday love — the little moments that quietly built you.
Your first adventures — the whole family, exploring the world together. 🛕
Caught mid-laugh, holding on tight — the joy you've carried your whole life.
Years pass, you grow up — but some arms always feel like home.
All grown up, still together — the family that made you, still right beside you.
Your very first home was her arms — and it still is. 💗
Last year, same love, brand-new smiles — your whole world in one frame.

memories.name: Her First Baby
memories.teaser: The first little one she ever loved like her own.
memories.message: He was the very first baby you ever loved like your own. Long before you were anyone's wife, you were his big sister — the one who mothered him, kissed his cheeks, carried him on your back, and decided early on that he was yours to protect. Watching how fiercely and tenderly you loved your little brother told me everything I needed to know about the heart you have. He was your first baby. And look at him now — all grown up, still laughing in your arms. Some bonds only grow stronger with time. 🧡

photos.caption: A kiss on the cheek and a bond for life.
Two against the world, right from the very start.
Carrying him everywhere — you never once put him down. 🧡
The kind of love that needs no words — just a cheek to kiss.
Reaching for the sky together — some hands you never stop holding.
All grown up now — and still, you're the one who makes him laugh like that. 🧡
Your baby brother — taller than you now, but forever your first little one. 🧡

memories.name: Becoming You
memories.teaser: A little girl becoming the woman I'd adore.
memories.message: Watch a little girl become a young woman, frame by frame. That same smile, those same shining eyes — they were always there, just growing into the person you were meant to be. Dancing like no one was watching, chasing the sea, laughing at something just out of frame: independent, fearless, completely your own. You never needed anyone to complete you, Nidhi — you were already whole, already brilliant, already everything. And every single one of these years, every version of you, was quietly leading you straight to the day our roads would finally cross.

photos.caption: That smile. Even then, it could light up an entire room.
A little older now — the same light in your eyes.
Somewhere in this face is the woman I'd fall for — I just didn't know it yet. ✨
Dancing like the whole world was yours — because it was. 💃
Just you and the sea — free, fearless, completely yourself. 🌊
That quiet little smile — the one I'd fall for without even knowing it yet.

memories.name: The Friends Who Became Family
memories.when: The ones who walked beside her
memories.teaser: Laughter, road trips, and friendships that became family.
memories.message: Everyone has a few people who feel like home — and these are yours. The girls who laughed with you until it hurt, travelled with you, kept your secrets and held your hand through everything life threw your way. The way you love your people — so loyally, so fully, so forever — was one of the very first things I admired about you. I'll always be grateful to the friends who loved you, shaped you, and helped make you the incredible woman who one day walked into my life.

photos.caption: The kind of friendship that needs no occasion — just the two of you, always.
Your girls — laughter guaranteed, every single time.
Sun, sand, and your favourite people. Some days are just pure joy. ☀️
Cheek to cheek, smiles all the way — the friends who feel like home.
No reason needed, just the two of you and a camera. 💛
Mirror selfies and shopping sprees — some friendships are forever in style. 🛍️

memories.name: Where It All Began
memories.teaser: A Kwid, a terrible coffee, and the start of everything.
memories.message: March 2022. I drove my little Renault Kwid to Bada Ganpati to pick you up — you'd just finished office and parked your Activa. We went on a long drive through the Super Corridor, windows down, talking like we'd known each other forever. At Cafe Yolo, I tried to impress you by ordering something called 'Cafe Anaconda' — easily the worst coffee either of us has ever tasted. But you laughed, and I knew. Later, when I had a short work call, I gently caressed your head — and you went completely calm. That was the moment I realised: I never want to stop being the person who makes your world feel quieter. Every single day since has been proof that I was right.

photos.caption: Bada Ganpati — where I picked you up that evening
The Kwid — our ride that started it all
Cafe Yolo — and the legendary Cafe Anaconda

quiz.q: What terrible coffee did I order at Cafe Yolo to impress you on our first meet?
quiz.right: Cafe Anaconda! Terrible coffee, perfect evening. The beginning of us. 💛
quiz.wrong: Not quite — the name was dramatic, the taste was awful, and the company was everything. Try again, my love.

memories.name: Our Roka
memories.teaser: Making it official, with blessings and love.
memories.message: The day we made it official. We went to the gurudwara together, heads bowed, hearts full — and then celebrated over lunch at Cafe Oakaz. It was simple, beautiful, and perfectly us. No grand gestures, just two people quietly promising to spend their lives together. That day, I looked at you across the table and thought: every road in my life was always leading here, to you.

photos.caption: The room that held our promise — balloons, petals, and us
Holding hands, holding futures
Celebrating our roka together
The laughs that made it all real
Rose petals, balloons, and the start of forever

memories.name: The Night I Proposed
memories.teaser: One knee, one slideshow, one chocolate box.
memories.message: I'd been planning this for weeks. The Eighteen restaurant, a custom slideshow playing on the big screen — every photo of us, every moment, building up to the question I already knew the answer to. When I got down on one knee and held out that chocolate box that said 'Will you marry me?' — Nidhi, the look on your face is burned into my heart forever. You said yes, and in that moment, I became the luckiest man alive. I still am.

photos.caption: The evening that changed everything — rooftop under fairy lights
That smile — she had no idea what was coming
Heart hands — if only she knew what I had planned
The moment right before I asked the most important question
The Eighteen — where I asked her to be mine forever
Together at The Eighteen — the night she said yes
Will U Marry Me — the chocolate box that sealed the deal

quiz.q: What was written on the chocolate box I gave you when I proposed?
quiz.right: 'Will you marry me?' — and you said yes. The best answer I've ever heard. 💍
quiz.wrong: Close but not quite — think chocolate, think a very specific question on the box. Try again, my love.

memories.name: The Day She Proposed Back
memories.teaser: My birthday, her chaos, one perfect ring.
memories.message: My birthday, March 2024 — and you had the sweetest secret plan. You wanted to propose somewhere truly special, somewhere grand and worthy of the moment. But I kept changing my birthday plans — one idea after another — and your perfect plan kept slipping right through your fingers. So with barely any time and your whole heart, you made a split-second decision: you pulled me up to the top floor of Cafe Oakaz (freshly renovated and gorgeous) and proposed to me right there, on the spot. It wasn't the venue you'd dreamed of — but Nidhi, it was perfect. Because in that instant I understood: you don't just love me, you adapt for us, you fight for us, even against my own chaotic, indecisive birthday self. I'd choose that messy, beautiful, instant 'yes' a thousand times over.

photos.caption: Cafe Oakaz — where her backup plan turned out perfect
The moment she made my heart stop
The top floor, just the two of us
Together at Oakaz — where she turned my birthday into forever
Holding hands, holding on to this moment

quiz.q: Why was it so chaotic for Nidhi to plan her proposal on my birthday?
options: I kept changing my birthday plans
She forgot the ring at home
quiz.right: I kept changing plans! Poor Nidhi was silently going mad trying to keep her surprise alive. Worth it. 💗
quiz.wrong: Not this one — think about whose birthday it was and how unpredictable the birthday boy was being. Try again!

memories.name: The Day We Said Forever
memories.message: 11th July 2024. The day I married the most beautiful woman in the world. Nidhi, you managed everything — every detail, every flower, every moment — and still looked absolutely breathtaking. You danced in my baarat, just for me, and I couldn't look anywhere else. Your mother, your mausi, and your cousins gave the most beautiful dance performances to welcome me, and I felt so loved by your whole family. And then, during the mala exchange, I couldn't help myself — I had a short little dance on stage to 'Tu Hai To Dil Dhadakta Hai.' Because my heart really was doing exactly that. It still does. Every single day.

photos.caption: Walking into forever — our grand entry with sparklers
On stage, jaimala done — the moment it all became real
The most beautiful bride I've ever seen — and my little dance
Seven rounds, one promise — by the sacred fire
First temple visit as Mr. and Mrs. — blessings for the road ahead

quiz.q: What song did I dance to on stage during the mala exchange at our wedding?
options: Tu Hai To Dil Dhadakta Hai
quiz.right: 'Tu Hai To Dil Dhadakta Hai' — because with you, it really does. Every day. 💒
quiz.wrong: Not that one — think about what my heart was doing the moment I put the mala around your neck. Try again!

memories.name: Bali & Malaysia
memories.message: Malaysia AND Bali — our honeymoon was an epic double feature. We ate thepla in the airport like proper Indori newlyweds, then spent the next days chasing every thrill we could find. The ATV ride through the Balinese jungle was wild, snorkeling made us feel like we'd discovered a secret ocean, and the dolphin centre — Nidhi, seeing your face when your childhood dream came true — I'll carry that forever. We lived on acai bowls, mango acai bowls, and drank from coconuts bigger than our heads by the beach. And somehow, in between all the adventure, we found the most incredible Indian food abroad. Two newlyweds, sandy feet, impossibly full hearts. This is where our forever really began.

photos.caption: Passports out — the adventure begins
Boarding our honeymoon flight
That beach, that sunset, you
Café mornings in Bali
Jungle ATV adventure
Making friends with dolphins
Helmets on, exploring Bali two-wheeler style
Standing tall at KL Tower
Taking in the KL skyline
Dinner dates in Kuala Lumpur
Under the Petronas Towers — Malaysia, hand in hand
That ocean view walkway — paradise found
Posing with the ancient stone guardians

quiz.q: What was Nidhi's childhood dream that came true on our honeymoon?
quiz.right: The dolphin centre! Seeing your face light up was the best part of the whole trip. 🐬
quiz.wrong: Not this one — think about something Nidhi had dreamed about since she was little. Try again, my love.

memories.name: Goa
memories.message: This one's tender. We came to Goa to heal — together. We partied on Tito's Street, took long drives all the way to Arambol with the windows down, stayed right by the beach where we could hear the waves at night. Neither of us knows how to swim — but that never stopped us for a second. We spent hours bathing in the ocean, laughing as the waves knocked us over, splashing about like two kids without a single worry in the world. We didn't need to swim; we just needed the water, and each other. We came carrying something heavy, and we left a little lighter. You reminded me that the right person makes even the hard chapters feel survivable. I quietly, completely fell in love with you all over again here.

photos.caption: On the flight — couldn't stop looking at each other
The sea that healed us
Ocean bath — just us and the waves
Watching the sunset, side by side
A kiss as the sun went down
Her, the sunset, and my whole world in one frame
Our beachside stay
Dancing on the beach at night
She owns the night
The butterfly wall — our spot

quiz.q: Goa wasn't just a holiday — what did we really do there?
options: Healed and found our way back to each other
quiz.right: We healed there, side by side. I'll never forget what that meant. 🩵
quiz.wrong: Gently no — it was something far more important than any activity. 🩵

memories.name: Snowops — Our Company
memories.message: Most couples share a Netflix account. We started a company. Snowops — built from late-night conversations, shared ambitions, and the wild belief that we could create something real, together. You are my co-founder in every sense: in business, in life, in dreaming bigger than either of us could alone. Building Snowops with you isn't just work — it's proof that when two people trust each other completely, there's nothing they can't build.

photos.caption: The beginning of Snowops
Co-founders in life and everything else

memories.name: Jaipur
memories.teaser: Temples and forts, hand in hand.
memories.message: The Pink City, hand in hand — but it was the temples that stole this trip. We visited Khatu Shyam Mandir and Salasar Balaji Mandir, and Nidhi, watching you there — the peace on your face, the devotion — I fell in love with this side of you, too. You absolutely loved it. We climbed the forts, explored every corner, and ate incredible food at Johari. Walking those golden walls beside you, I kept thinking how lucky I am that I get to explore this entire life with you.

photos.caption: Together at Amber Fort
Hawa Mahal lit up at night
Dreaming by Jal Mahal
The Pink City sparkling below
Our royal palace moment
Mirror selfie at our Rajasthani haveli

quiz.q: Which two temples did we visit on our Jaipur trip that Nidhi absolutely loved?
options: Khatu Shyam Mandir and Salasar Balaji Mandir
quiz.right: Khatu Shyam and Salasar Balaji! The peace on your face there is something I'll never forget. 🙏
quiz.wrong: Not those — think about the two temples where Nidhi felt the most at home. Try again!

memories.name: Our Home
memories.teaser: We explored every corner of the city — and found home.
memories.message: We walked through so many areas, debated so many floor plans, imagined so many futures — and finally, we found it. Our flat. Our first real home, just for us. No more temporary, no more 'someday.' This is where we'll cook our first meal together, where we'll argue about wall colours, where we'll build the life we've been dreaming about since that first drive down the Super Corridor. Every room in this flat is a promise. And I can't wait to fill it with us.

photos.caption: Coming soon — the next chapter of us

memories.name: Indore — Home
memories.teaser: Home — our everyday love, one long drive at a time.
memories.message: Not every love story needs a passport. Our own city holds our smallest, sweetest moments — and the truth is, our favourite thing was never a café or a fancy table. It was the open road: long, aimless drives in our little Kwid, windows down, music up, talking about everything and nothing. Yes, we ate together at plenty of places — but we were never the crazy-about-cafés kind. No restaurant in Indore knows us by name, none of them remember 'our order.' We were always more about the drive than the destination. Home isn't a place, Nidhi — it's the passenger seat right next to me.

photos.caption: Dressed up, showing up — always together
Holi with family — colours, chaos, and us
My Burger King queen
Our favourite corner of every café
Coffee and sunshine — her happy place
That smile across the table
Free spirit on the tracks
The hand-kiss that stopped time

quiz.q: What was our absolute favourite thing to do together in Indore?
options: Long, aimless drives in our Kwid
quiz.right: Long drives in our little Kwid — windows down, music up, nowhere to be. Our happy place. 🚗
quiz.wrong: Close, but think simpler — windows down, open road, just us and the Kwid. Try again, my love!

memories.name: A Birthday Promise
memories.teaser: A promise for the year ahead — and every year after.
memories.message:
My dearest Nidhi,

Every card before this one is a piece of your story — from the tiny girl who arrived and changed the world, to little you blowing out birthday candles, to the years that quietly shaped you — and then to us: that first drive in my Kwid to Cafe Yolo, our roka at the gurudwara, to the night I proposed at The Eighteen, to the day you proposed right back at Cafe Oakaz, to our wedding on 11th July when you danced in my baarat, to our honeymoon in Bali where your dolphin dream came true, to every trip, every meal, every quiet moment that made us us.

We built Snowops together. We found our flat together. And now, on your birthday, I want you to know: you are the bravest, most brilliant person I've ever met. A lawyer, a company secretary, a dreamer who chose to bet on us and build something beautiful together.

So here's my promise for this year and every year after:

I promise to be your safest place when the world feels loud. I promise to cook with you, travel with you, laugh with you until our cheeks hurt. I promise to celebrate you — not just today, but on every ordinary Tuesday, every tired evening, every quiet morning.

You are not just my wife. You are my favourite person, my best adventure, my home.

Happy birthday, Nidhi. This is just the beginning.
— Forever and always yours.
memories.ps: P.S. You just proved you know our story by heart. But here's a secret only I know: every single day with you has been my favourite day — right up until the next one. Now close your eyes, make a wish… and come find me. 🥂
memories.birthdayNote:
This year, I wish for you: a thousand more sunsets together, every flavour you've ever craved, and the kind of happiness that makes your heart feel too full to hold.

And here's what I see when I dream about our future:

Travelling the world together — every continent, every culture, every sunset we haven't seen yet. Buying great cars — because you deserve to arrive in style (and I deserve a co-pilot who controls the playlist). Enjoying life — really, truly, deeply. Building Snowops into something we're both proud of. And most of all: growing old together, still laughing, still adventuring, still falling in love with you every morning.

You deserve the whole world, Nidhi — and I'm going to spend my life making sure you feel it. Happy birthday, my love. 💛

photos.caption: Live life, queen size

feastPhotos.caption: Breakfast served in a coconut, the ocean for company — Bali spoiled us 🥥
Fine-dining art on a plate — almost too pretty to disturb
Candle-lit dinner abroad — handmade crepes and a fragrant curry, just for two
A grand tandoori platter on a banana leaf — paneer tikka, kebabs and all
Malai kofta in velvety tomato gravy — comfort food that tastes like home
A royal Rajasthani thali — dal baati churma and a dozen little bowls of joy
Bao buns lined up and dressed to impress — gone in minutes
Tangy mocktails and a sizzling stir-fry — an evening, unplugged
A wood-fired pizza loaded with peppers and jalapeños — our kind of cheesy
A loaded sandwich, a mountain of fries, and every sauce within reach
A burger almost too pretty to eat — purple bun, crispy wedges, a dunk of sauce
Lazy café mornings — a croissant, a cheese puff, and the coldest coffee
Street-side sweetness eaten on the move — cherries, custard, zero regrets
Christmas-tree churros under a waterfall of warm chocolate — need I say more?
Mango mocktail and tiny bites — Jaipur
Desserts on a banana leaf — Jaipur
Chaat so good we almost cried — Jaipur
Naan, curry, and no regrets — Jaipur
The Rajasthani thali of dreams — Jaipur
Thali round two with lassi — Jaipur
Poori-kachori breakfast — Jaipur

loveQuotes: In all the world, there is no heart for me like yours.
Every love story is beautiful, but ours is my favourite.
I choose you. And I'll choose you over and over.
You are my today and all of my tomorrows.
Wherever you are is my home.
I loved you yesterday. I love you still. I always have, I always will.
You are my sun, my moon, and all my stars.
From Cafe Yolo to forever — I'd choose this story every time.
You danced in my baarat. I'll dance for you every day.
Co-founders in love, in life, in everything.

reasons.reason: The way you laugh — really laugh — with your whole heart
How you make every meal feel like a celebration
Your courage to chase every dream, no matter how big
The way you light up when you talk about something you love
How you always know exactly what to say to make everything okay
Your strength — you are the bravest person I know
The way you look at me like I'm your favourite person in any room
How you turn every trip into the best adventure of my life
Your kindness — the way you treat everyone with so much warmth
The little notes, the little touches, the little ways you love me
How you challenge me to be a better version of myself every day
The fact that you chose me — and keep choosing me
Your smile first thing in the morning
The way you make our house feel like home
How you never give up on anything — especially us
Your voice when you call my name
The way you steal all the blankets and I don't even mind
How every photo of us is my new favourite photo
Your passion for food — and for sharing it with me
The way you make even ordinary days feel extraordinary
How safe I feel when I'm with you
Your determination — lawyer, company secretary, dreamer, all at once
How you kept your proposal a secret even when I kept changing plans
The way you managed our entire wedding and still looked like a dream
How you light up at temples — Khatu Shyam, Salasar Balaji, Nathdwara
Your courage to start Snowops with me — co-founder of my whole life
The way you calmed down when I touched your head at Cafe Yolo — and I knew
Our long, aimless drives in the Kwid — windows down, just you and me
The way you jumped into the ocean with me, even though neither of us can swim
The way you hug me tighter when you know I've had a hard day
How you dance like the whole world is yours — and it is
Because you are my today, my tomorrow, and every day after

secretMessages: The first coffee I ever ordered for you was 'Cafe Anaconda.' Still embarrassed. ☕
Remember how you calmed down when I touched your head at Cafe Yolo? That's when I knew. 🤍
You kept your proposal secret while I kept changing plans. You're incredible. 💍
We ate thepla in the airport on our honeymoon. Most Indori thing ever. 🫓
The day you danced in my baarat — I couldn't see anyone else. 💃
From a Kwid and an Activa to our own flat and our own company. Look how far we've come. 🏠
Every new day with you is still my favourite chapter. 💛

promises.text: Cook your favourite dish from scratch
Go on a long, aimless Kwid drive at sunset
Watch the sunrise together at least once
Build our home into the warmest place on earth
Surprise you when you least expect it
Dance with you in the kitchen
Hold your hand through every hard day
Love you louder, every single day
